content: To whom did the angel announce the birth of Jesus?
explanation: Luke 1:26-38 — The angel Gabriel announced the news to Mary.

content: Who first received the angels' good news of the Nativity?
explanation: Luke 2:8-14 — The shepherds heard the angel announce the good news.

content: When Jesus was baptized, the Holy Spirit descended in what form?
explanation: Luke 3:22 — The Holy Spirit descended in bodily form like a dove.

content: What miracle made Peter fall to his knees, saying 'Depart from me, Lord'?
explanation: Luke 5:6-8 — The miraculous catch of fish made Peter tremble before holiness.

content: What did Jesus teach about enemies?
explanation: Luke 6:27 — 'Love your enemies, do good to those who hate you.'

content: What is the model prayer Jesus taught called?
explanation: Luke 11:2-4 — The prayer: 'Our Father in heaven...'

content: What did the sinful woman do to Jesus' feet at Simon's house?
explanation: Luke 7:38 — Wept on them, wiped with her hair, kissed, and anointed with perfume.
options: Wet them with tears, wiped with her hair, anointed with oil

content: In the parable of the sower, what happened to the seeds that fell on the path?
explanation: Luke 8:5,12 — Birds devoured them; the evil one snatches the word from the heart.

content: Who appeared at the Transfiguration in Luke?
explanation: Luke 9:30 — The two men were Moses and Elijah.

content: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, who helped the man in need?
explanation: Luke 10:33 — The Samaritan saw and had compassion.

content: How did Jesus teach about anxiety over food and clothing?
explanation: Luke 12:29-31 — Seek first the kingdom of God.
options: Do not worry; first seek the kingdom of God

content: In the lesson about the seat at the feast, what does the Lord teach?
explanation: Luke 14:10 — Take the lowest place, so the host may say 'Friend, move up higher.'

content: In the parable of the prodigal son, what does the father do when the son returns?
explanation: Luke 15:20-24 — The father had compassion, ran out, embraced and kissed him, threw a feast.
options: Ran out, embraced and kissed him, threw a feast

content: In the story of the rich man and Lazarus, where were they after death?
explanation: Luke 16:22-23 — The rich man was in torment; Lazarus was at Abraham's side.
options: The rich man in torment, Lazarus at Abraham's side

content: What does the parable of the unjust judge teach about prayer?
explanation: Luke 18:1 — He told them they ought always to pray and not lose heart.

content: Who was Zacchaeus and what did he do after meeting Jesus?
explanation: Luke 19:2,8 — Zacchaeus promised to repay fourfold and give to the poor.
options: A merchant who built a synagogue
Chief tax collector who restored fourfold and gave to the poor

content: How did Jesus answer about paying taxes to Caesar?
explanation: Luke 20:25 — A double principle of civic and divine responsibility.
options: Give it all to the temple
Render to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's

content: At Gethsemane, what did Jesus teach His disciples?
explanation: Luke 22:40 — Pray that you may not enter into temptation.
options: Pray that you may not enter into temptation

content: What did Jesus say to the repentant thief on the cross?
explanation: Luke 23:43 — A promise of paradise to the repentant.
options: Today you will be with Me in Paradise

content: When did the two disciples on the Emmaus road recognize Jesus?
explanation: Luke 24:30-31 — Their eyes were opened when He broke the bread.

content: In Luke 2:14, 'Glory to God in the highest.' What does the Greek word for 'glory' mean?
explanation: Doxa (δόξα) means 'glory, honor' — the splendor and majesty of God.

content: In Luke 15:20, 'The father ran and embraced him.' What does the Greek for 'ran' mean?
explanation: Dramo (δραμών) means 'running quickly,' showing the father's haste and love.

content: Where was Jesus born?
explanation: Luke 2:7 — Mary gave birth to her firstborn son, wrapped Him in cloths, and laid Him in a manger because there was no room in the inn.

content: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, who helped the man who had been robbed?
explanation: Luke 10:33-34 — The Samaritan (despised by the Jews) was the only one who showed compassion and helped.

content: In the parable of the lost sheep, how many sheep did the shepherd have before losing one?
explanation: Luke 15:4 — The shepherd had 100, lost 1, left the 99 to go after the lost one. Illustrates God's love for the sinner.

content: In the parable of the prodigal son, when the son returned, what did the father do?
explanation: Luke 15:20-24 — The father ran out and embraced him, put on the best robe, the ring, and held a feast. A beautiful image of God welcoming the repentant sinner.
options: Ran out, embraced him, and held a feast

content: What did Jesus promise the repentant thief on the cross?
explanation: Luke 23:43 — Salvation requires only sincere faith, not works. The thief simply believed and was promised paradise.
options: Today you will be with Me in Paradise

content: Jesus read Isaiah 61 at Nazareth and declared He came to do what?
explanation: Luke 4:18-19 — Jesus declared His mission using Isaiah's words: total liberation and healing.
options: Preach the gospel to the poor, heal the brokenhearted, free captives

content: Luke 6:38: '___ and it will be given to you; good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap'
explanation: The principle of generosity: give and you will receive abundantly. God blesses generous hearts.

content: Between Martha distracted with serving and Mary sitting and listening, whom did Jesus commend?
explanation: Luke 10:42 — Mary chose the better part: sitting at the Lord's feet to hear His Word. Relationship is greater than service.
options: Mary, for choosing the better part

content: Jesus warned: 'Take care, and be on your guard against all ___'
explanation: Luke 12:15 — True life does not consist in possessions. The lesson of the parable of the rich fool.
options: Covetousness, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions

content: Of the ten lepers healed, how many returned to give thanks?
explanation: Luke 17:15-16 — Only one of ten was thankful. Jesus asked: where are the other nine? A lesson on gratitude.
options: Only one (and he was a Samaritan)

content: In the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector, who was justified?
explanation: Luke 18:14 — The tax collector simply cried, 'God, be merciful to me, a sinner.' And was justified.
options: The Pharisee, for doing many good works
The tax collector, for humble repentance

content: What name did the angel Gabriel command Zechariah to give his son?
explanation: Luke 1:13 — 'Do not be afraid, Zechariah, for your prayer has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall call his name John.'

content: What name did the angel command Mary to give her child, and on whose throne would He sit?
explanation: Luke 1:31-32 — 'You will conceive in your womb and bear a son, and you shall call his name Jesus... and the Lord God will give to him the throne of his father David.'

content: How did Mary respond to the angel?
explanation: Luke 1:38 — 'And Mary said, Behold, I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word.'
options: I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word

content: When Mary visited Elizabeth, how did Elizabeth greet her?
explanation: Luke 1:42 — 'Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb!'
options: Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb

content: What song did Mary sing in praise of God (the Magnificat)?
explanation: Luke 1:46-48 — 'My soul magnifies the Lord, and my spirit rejoices in God my Savior, for he has looked on the humble estate of his servant.'

content: Which emperor issued the decree for a census that brought Joseph and Mary to Bethlehem?
explanation: Luke 2:1 — 'In those days a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.'

content: Why did Mary lay the infant Jesus in a manger?
explanation: Luke 2:7 — 'And she gave birth to her firstborn son and wrapped him in swaddling cloths and laid him in a manger, because there was no place for them in the inn.'
options: Because there was no place for them in the inn

content: What song did the heavenly host sing at Jesus' birth?
explanation: Luke 2:14 — 'Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace among those with whom he is pleased!'
options: The Christ is Lord, He has come
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace

content: Which old man at the temple held the infant Jesus and sang 'Lord, now you are letting your servant depart in peace'?
explanation: Luke 2:25-29 — 'Now there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simeon... he took him up in his arms and blessed God.'

content: Which prophetess, long widowed, fasted and prayed daily in the temple and met the infant Jesus?
explanation: Luke 2:36-37 — 'And there was a prophetess, Anna, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher. She was advanced in years.'

content: At age 12, where was Jesus found, and what did He say to His parents?
explanation: Luke 2:46,49 — 'After three days they found him in the temple, sitting among the teachers... Why were you looking for me? Did you not know that I must be in my Father's house?'
options: In the temple — I must be in My Father's house

content: When Jesus was baptized, in what form did the Holy Spirit descend on Him?
explanation: Luke 3:22 — 'And the Holy Spirit descended on him in bodily form, like a dove.'

content: Luke's genealogy of Jesus traces back to which forefather?
explanation: Luke 3:38 — '...the son of Adam, the son of God.' Luke traces back to Adam (Matthew traces only to Abraham).

content: At the Nazareth synagogue, Jesus read Isaiah 61: 'The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because He has ___'
explanation: Luke 4:18 — 'The Spirit of the Lord is upon me, because he has anointed me to proclaim good news to the poor. He has sent me to proclaim liberty to the captives and recovering of sight to the blind.'
options: Anointed Me — to proclaim good news to the poor, heal broken-hearted, free captives, give sight to blind
Sent Me to build the temple
Sent Me to judge the world
Sent Me to teach the law

content: What did Jesus say about a prophet in his hometown?
explanation: Luke 4:24 — 'Truly, I say to you, no prophet is acceptable in his hometown.'
options: A prophet is most honored at home
A prophet is praised by the people
No prophet is acceptable in his hometown

content: When Jesus told Peter to let down the nets, how did Peter respond?
explanation: Luke 5:5 — 'Master, we toiled all night and took nothing! But at your word I will let down the nets.'
options: I don't believe there are fish
We toiled all night and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the nets

content: After the miraculous catch, how did Peter react?
explanation: Luke 5:8 — 'But when Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord.'
options: Fell down and said: Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord
Stood up and went to sell the fish

content: Whom did Jesus say He came to call to repentance?
explanation: Luke 5:32 — 'I have not come to call the righteous but sinners to repentance.'

content: The Sermon on the Plain (Luke) begins with blessings on whom?
explanation: Luke 6:20-21 — 'Blessed are you who are poor, for yours is the kingdom of God. Blessed are you who are hungry now, for you shall be satisfied. Blessed are you who weep now, for you shall laugh.'
options: Blessed are the righteous, obedient, gentle
Blessed are the priests, prophets, kings
Blessed are the poor, the hungry, those who weep

content: What does the Golden Rule in Luke 6:31 teach?
explanation: Luke 6:31 — 'And as you wish that others would do to you, do so to them.'
options: Treat others as they treat you
Do to others as you would have them do to you

content: Luke 6:38 — '___, and it will be given to you; with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.'
explanation: Luke 6:38 — 'Give, and it will be given to you. Good measure, pressed down, shaken together, running over, will be put into your lap.'
tags: Give and It Will Be Given

content: In which town did Jesus raise the only son of a widow?
explanation: Luke 7:11-15 — 'Soon afterward he went to a town called Nain... he came up and touched the bier... Young man, I say to you, arise. And the dead man sat up.'

content: What did the sinful woman do to Jesus' feet at Simon the Pharisee's house?
explanation: Luke 7:38 — 'And standing behind him at his feet, weeping, she began to wet his feet with her tears and wiped them with the hair of her head and kissed his feet and anointed them with the ointment.'
options: Wept, wet His feet with tears, wiped with hair, kissed and anointed with oil

content: Which women does Luke 8:1-3 mention as following and providing for Jesus from their means?
explanation: Luke 8:2-3 — 'Mary, called Magdalene, from whom seven demons had gone out, and Joanna, the wife of Chuza, Herod's household manager, and Susanna, and many others.'
options: Mary Magdalene, Joanna wife of Chuza, Susanna and many others
Mary mother of Jesus and Elizabeth

content: When Jesus arrived at Jairus's house, what did He say to the dead daughter?
explanation: Luke 8:54 — 'Taking her by the hand he called, saying, Child, arise.'

content: What did Jesus teach a follower must do 'daily'?
explanation: Luke 9:23 — 'If anyone would come after me, let him deny himself and take up his cross daily and follow me.'
options: Deny self, take up cross daily, and follow Me

content: Jesus said: 'Foxes have holes, birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to ___'
explanation: Luke 9:58 — 'Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.'

content: Luke 9:62 — What does Jesus say of those who put their hand to the plow and look back?
explanation: Luke 9:62 — 'No one who puts his hand to the plow and looks back is fit for the kingdom of God.'
options: Is not fit for the kingdom of God

content: How many disciples did Jesus send out two by two to proclaim the gospel?
explanation: Luke 10:1 — 'After this the Lord appointed seventy-two others and sent them on ahead of him, two by two, into every town and place where he himself was about to go.' (Some manuscripts: 70)

content: In the parable of the Good Samaritan, which three people passed the wounded man?
explanation: Luke 10:31-33 — 'Now by chance a priest was going down that road... So likewise a Levite... But a Samaritan, as he journeyed, came to where he was.'
options: A king, a noble, a farmer
A Pharisee, a scribe, a disciple
A priest, a Levite, a Samaritan (the Good)

content: At Bethany, who chose 'the good portion' by sitting at Jesus' feet?
explanation: Luke 10:42 — 'But one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen the good portion, which will not be taken away from her.'

content: When the disciples asked Jesus to teach them to pray, how does the prayer begin?
explanation: Luke 11:2 — 'Father, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come.'
options: Father, hallowed be your name; your kingdom come

content: Luke 11:9 — '___, and it will be given; ___, and you will find; ___, and it will be opened.'
explanation: Luke 11:9 — 'Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.'

content: Luke 12:7 — What does Jesus say about the hairs on the disciples' heads?
explanation: Luke 12:7 — 'Even the hairs of your head are all numbered. Fear not; you are of more value than many sparrows.'
options: The hairs will all fall out
The hairs are evidence of sin
The hairs are all numbered; you are worth more than many sparrows
The hairs will turn white from worry

content: Luke 12:15 — A person's life does not consist in what?
explanation: Luke 12:15 — 'Take care, and be on your guard against all covetousness, for one's life does not consist in the abundance of his possessions.'
options: In the abundance of his possessions

content: Jesus said to the little flock: 'Fear not, for ___'
explanation: Luke 12:32 — 'Fear not, little flock, for it is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom.'
options: It is your Father's good pleasure to give you the kingdom
I will give you the promised land

content: Luke 12:48 — Of one to whom much is given, what will be required?
explanation: Luke 12:48 — 'Everyone to whom much was given, of him much will be required, and from him to whom they entrusted much, they will demand the more.'
options: Much will be required, and the more demanded

content: Jesus healed a woman who had been bent over by a spirit for how many years?
explanation: Luke 13:11 — 'There was a woman who had had a disabling spirit for eighteen years. She was bent over and could not fully straighten herself.'

content: Luke 13:24 — Jesus taught one must strive to do what to enter the kingdom of God?
explanation: Luke 13:24 — 'Strive to enter through the narrow door. For many, I tell you, will seek to enter and will not be able.'

content: Luke 14:11 — What happens to the one who exalts himself, and to the one who humbles himself?
explanation: Luke 14:11 — 'For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted.'
options: The one who exalts is praised / the humble is forgotten
The one who exalts is rich / the humble is poor
Everyone who exalts will be humbled, and he who humbles himself will be exalted

content: Luke 14:27 — Whoever does not bear his cross and follow Jesus cannot be what?
explanation: Luke 14:27 — 'Whoever does not bear his own cross and come after me cannot be my disciple.'

content: Luke 15:7 — In heaven there is what kind of joy?
explanation: Luke 15:7 — 'There will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents than over ninety-nine righteous persons who need no repentance.'
options: Over one sinner who repents — more than over 99 righteous who need no repentance
When a nation returns to God

content: In the parable of the lost coin, how many coins did the woman have, and how many were lost?
explanation: Luke 15:8 — 'What woman, having ten silver coins, if she loses one coin, does not light a lamp and sweep the house and seek diligently until she finds it?'
options: A few coins, a few lost

content: Luke 16:13 — No one can serve two masters; you cannot serve God and ___
explanation: Luke 16:13 — 'You cannot serve God and money [Mammon].'

content: In the parable of the rich man and Lazarus, what did the rich man ask Abraham to send Lazarus to do?
explanation: Luke 16:24 — 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the end of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am in anguish in this flame.'
options: Bring him over to Abraham's side
Dip his finger in water and cool his tongue (because he was tormented in flame)

content: Of the ten lepers cleansed, how many returned to give thanks, and what was the man's nationality?
explanation: Luke 17:15-16 — 'Then one of them, when he saw that he was healed, turned back, praising God with a loud voice... Now he was a Samaritan.'

content: Luke 17:21 — Where is the kingdom of God?
explanation: Luke 17:21 — 'Nor will they say, Look, here it is! or There! For behold, the kingdom of God is in the midst of you.'

content: What four short words did Jesus speak about looking back?
explanation: Luke 17:32 — 'Remember Lot's wife.' (She looked back and was turned to a pillar of salt.)

content: Luke 18:1 — Jesus told the parable of the widow and judge to teach what?
explanation: Luke 18:1 — 'And he told them a parable to the effect that they ought always to pray and not lose heart.'
options: Always pray and not lose heart

content: In the parable of the Pharisee and the tax collector praying in the temple, who went home 'justified'?
explanation: Luke 18:14 — 'I tell you, this man (the tax collector) went down to his house justified, rather than the other (the Pharisee).'
options: The tax collector — for beating his breast in repentance

content: Which tax collector climbed a tree to see Jesus and had Him visit his house?
explanation: Luke 19:2-5 — 'There was a man named Zacchaeus. He was a chief tax collector and was rich. And he was seeking to see who Jesus was, but on account of the crowd he could not, because he was small in stature. So he ran on ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.'

content: Luke 19:10 — Why did the Son of Man come?
explanation: Luke 19:10 — 'For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.'
options: To seek and to save the lost
To teach the law of Moses

content: Luke 20:25 — Render to Caesar what is Caesar's, and render to whom what is whose?
explanation: Luke 20:25 — 'Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.'
options: To the priest things of God
To the people things of the king
To God the things that are God's

content: Luke 21:1-4 — What did the poor widow put into the offering box that Jesus praised?
explanation: Luke 21:2-4 — 'And he saw a poor widow put in two small copper coins... For they all contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty put in all she had to live on.'
options: Two small copper coins — all she had to live on

content: Luke 21:33 — Heaven and earth will pass away, but what will not pass away?
explanation: Luke 21:33 — 'Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.'

content: Luke 22:19 — Jesus took bread and said: 'This is my body... ___'
explanation: Luke 22:19 — 'This is my body, which is given for you. Do this in remembrance of me.'
options: Do this in remembrance of Me

content: At Gethsemane, Jesus prayed: 'Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but ___'
explanation: Luke 22:42 — 'Father, if you are willing, remove this cup from me. Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done.'

content: Luke 22:44 — In great anguish, what was Jesus' sweat like?
explanation: Luke 22:44 — 'And being in an agony he prayed more earnestly; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.'
options: Like great drops of blood falling to the ground

content: When Peter cut off the servant's ear, what did Jesus do?
explanation: Luke 22:51 — 'But Jesus said, No more of this! And he touched his ear and healed him.'
options: Touched the servant's ear and healed him

content: After Peter denied Jesus a third time, the Lord turned and looked at him; how did Peter react?
explanation: Luke 22:61-62 — 'And the Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered the saying of the Lord... And he went out and wept bitterly.'

content: On the cross, what was Jesus' first saying according to Luke 23:34?
explanation: Luke 23:34 — 'Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.'
options: Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do
Father, into your hands I commit my spirit

content: Jesus said to the thief on the cross: '___'
explanation: Luke 23:43 — 'Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.'
options: Today you will be with Me in paradise

content: What were Jesus' final words according to Luke 23:46?
explanation: Luke 23:46 — 'Then Jesus, calling out with a loud voice, said, Father, into your hands I commit my spirit! And having said this he breathed his last.'
options: Father, into your hands I commit my spirit

content: The risen Jesus appeared to two disciples on the road to where?
explanation: Luke 24:13 — 'That very day two of them were going to a village named Emmaus, about seven miles from Jerusalem.'

content: On the road to Emmaus, from where did Jesus explain the Scriptures concerning Himself?
explanation: Luke 24:27 — 'And beginning with Moses and all the Prophets, he interpreted to them in all the Scriptures the things concerning himself.'
options: Beginning with Moses and all the Prophets — concerning Him in all the Scriptures

content: After His resurrection, how did Jesus prove He was not a ghost?
explanation: Luke 24:42-43 — 'They gave him a piece of broiled fish, and he took it and ate before them.'
options: Ate a piece of broiled fish before the disciples

content: Where did Jesus ascend, according to Luke?
explanation: Luke 24:50-51 — 'Then he led them out as far as Bethany, and lifting up his hands he blessed them. While he blessed them, he parted from them and was carried up into heaven.'
options: At Bethany — while He was blessing them
